name: Rack of Lamb Primeurs
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the chine bone and separate the rack into two halves.
2. Cut off the ends of the bones with a cleaver or saw and reserve for soup.
3. Trim off excess fat from the top of the meat, leaving a thin layer.
4. Place the bones at the bottom of a roasting pan.
5. Place the meat (fat side up) on top of the bones in the pan.
6. Season with salt, pepper, and thyme.
7. Roast at 450°F for 30 minutes, until rare to medium-rare.
8. Remove from the pan and keep warm. Leave the bones in the pan.
9. Set the pan over a moderate flame to caramelize the juices and clarify the fat.
10. Pour off the excess fat.
11. Add garlic to the pan and cook for 1 minute.
12. Add stock and stir to deglaze the pan, dissolving the fond. Heat until reduced by half.
13. Strain through a chinois, degreasing as needed.
14. Season to taste.
15. Pour the juices into a sauceboat.
16. Cut between ribs to create chops.
17. Place onto a heated platter and garnish with assorted vegetables.
18. Serve hot.
